title: Kairos 3.0: A Unified Cross-Embodiment World Model
summary: Kairos 3.0 is a unified cross-embodiment world model that uses physical and causal laws as its cognitive foundation. Its 4B-parameter architecture employs a custom hybrid linear attention operator to unify multimodal understanding, generation, and action prediction in a single end-to-end pipeline, integrating real-robot interaction, structured human behavior, and chain-of-thought data. The hybrid linear-attention operator cuts attention's temporal complexity from O(n^2) to O(n), which the authors say enables the first real-time on-robot inference for an open-source world model.
